name.text: Practice Q1: Splunk brute force query

questiontext.text: Write a Splunk SPL query to count the number of failed SSH login attempts grouped by source IP address. Use index=main and sourcetype=linux_secure.

answer.text: index=main sourcetype=linux_secure "Failed password" | rex "from (?<src_ip>\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+)" | stats count by src_ip

questiontext.text: List the four main stages of alert triage in order (one per line).

name.text: Practice Q3: Response to C2 DNS alert

questiontext.text: During the lab, an alert showed "DNS query to known malware C2 domain." What is the most critical immediate action a Tier 1 analyst should take?

answer.text: Escalate to Tier 2 and recommend isolating the affected machine
Isolate the affected machine and escalate

questiontext.text: Name three KPIs used to measure SOC performance (acronyms acceptable).

name.text: Practice Q5: SOAR vs SIEM difference

questiontext.text: In one sentence, what is the key difference between a SIEM and a SOAR platform?

answer.text: SIEM collects and correlates logs to detect and alert, while SOAR automates response workflows and orchestrates actions across tools.

name.text: Practice Q6: Alert triage scenario - account compromise

questiontext.text:
You are a Tier 1 analyst and receive the following alert:

User jsmith attempted to log in from IP 203.0.113.100 (geolocation: North Korea) at 03:15 AM. 15 failed attempts followed by 1 successful login. The user then accessed the HR database and downloaded 3,000 records.

Describe your complete triage process, including: your classification, severity assessment, actions taken, and what information you would include in your escalation to Tier 2. (150-250 words)

graderinfo.text:
Award full marks for answers that include:

  • True Positive / Critical severity classification
  • Context check (North Korea IP, 3AM, HR database)
  • Immediate containment: disable jsmith account, block IP
  • Preserve evidence: HR access logs
  • Escalation details: timeline, IP, data accessed, MITRE techniques
  • Recognition this is a potential data breach

name.text: Practice Q7: Alert fatigue - causes, consequences, mitigation

questiontext.text: Explain the concept of "alert fatigue" in a SOC environment. What causes it, what are its consequences, and what strategies can a SOC implement to mitigate it? Provide at least 3 specific mitigation strategies with justification. (200-300 words)

graderinfo.text:
Award full marks for answers that include:

  • Clear definition of alert fatigue
  • Causes: high volume, high false positive rate, poor tuning
  • Consequences: missed threats, analyst burnout, slower response
  • At least 3 mitigation strategies with reasoning (e.g., rule tuning, SOAR automation, alert prioritization)
